author: loglux
description: Convert LaTeX equations from ChatGPT and AI assistants to the correct Markdown format: block equations to display math, inline to inline math.

manifest.author: Vladislav Sorokin
manifest.description: Convert LaTeX equations from ChatGPT and AI assistants to Obsidian format: block equations to display math, inline to inline math.
manifest.name: Fix Math

name: Fix Math

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Support Korean grammatical particles after plain-parenthesis math expressions (e.g. (\lambda)와 → $\lambda$와). Also fixes two pre-existing ESLint warnings.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Treat [content] without spaces as text; only [ content ] with a leading space is considered a display math block. This naturally handles author-year citations like [Puterman, 1994] without special cases.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Support full-width CJK punctuation (，。！？；：、) after closing parenthesis

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: pure letter lists like [a, b] or [x, y, z] are no longer converted to display math blocks

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: pure numeric lists like [11, 46] are no longer converted to math blocks

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: escape unescaped # inside math blocks (ChatGPT 'number of' symbol)

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix adjacent \]\[ display math blocks on the same line not being converted

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: block math with nested brackets (e.g. \mathbf{1}[condition]) no longer closes prematurely. The bracket block handler now uses depth-counting instead of a regex to find the correct closing ].

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: brackets inside existing $...$ inline math (e.g. $x_1 = [1, 2, 3]$) are no longer incorrectly converted to display math blocks.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: square-bracket abbreviations like [RL], [ML] and footnote references like [1] are no longer incorrectly converted to display math. The bracket handler now uses stricter math detection that requires actual mathematical content.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Fix: inline code spans (single backticks) are now protected from math conversion. Content like `msg[layer_idx]` will no longer be incorrectly treated as math.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Fix: brackets inside \( \) were incorrectly converted to block math

Both plain [x] and \left[...\right] inside \( ... \) expressions are now preserved. Previously, \(\mathbb{R}[x]_{n+1}\) would be broken into \(\mathbb{R}$$\nx\n$$_{n+1}\). The bracket handlers now detect when they are inside a \( ... \) span and skip conversion.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Fix: parentheses inside \( \) were incorrectly converted

\( (a + b) + c = a + (b + c) \) now correctly becomes $(a + b) + c = a + (b + c)$ instead of the broken $$a + b$ + c = a + $b + c$$. The plain-parentheses scanner now skips over \( ... \) spans entirely, leaving them for the dedicated backslash handler.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Selection mode: convert only selected text

When text is selected before running the command, only the selection is converted. Without a selection, the whole file is processed as before.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Convert geometry notation and single-letter variables to inline math

- (DEM), (EM), (ABC), (M) — geometry points/segments/triangles now convert
- (x), (n), (p) — single-letter variables now convert
- (\angle DEM=40^\circ) — LaTeX with geometry labels now converts (hasLaTeXCommand guard was missing from convertPlainParens)
- Uppercase-only sequences bypass natural language filter (changed \p{L}{3,} to \p{Ll}{3,})

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Fix: preserve nested brackets inside converted block math (1.0.18)

When a multiline block like:

  [
  \mathbf{X} = [\mathbf{1}\ \mathbf{S}]
  ]

was converted, the inner [\mathbf{1}\ \mathbf{S}] was mistakenly converted to a separate display math block, breaking the formula. Now the outer block becomes a single dollar-dollar block and inner brackets are left intact.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
**Fix: ChatGPT matrix export artefacts (1.0.17)**

Improves handling of malformed math content exported from ChatGPT into Obsidian with 'Convert HTML to Markdown' disabled.

**Fixed inside display math blocks:**
- '=====' on its own line becomes '=' (setext-heading artefact for equality sign)
- '-----' on its own line becomes '-' (horizontal-rule artefact for minus sign)
- '## formula' at line start: heading prefix removed, '-' appended (marks a subtracted term)
- '+,formula' / '-,formula' become '+formula' / '-formula' (comma-separator artefact)
- Single backslash at end of matrix row becomes double backslash (broken row separator)
- Single backslash before digit becomes double backslash (compact column vector artefact)

**Tip:** For best results, disable 'Convert HTML content to Markdown on paste' in Obsidian Settings > Editor before pasting ChatGPT responses.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
- Fix: correct conversion for [ \left[ ... \right]_{x=0} ] blocks
  - Improve: convert single-letter vars in parentheses (p) → $p$
  - Fix: parentheses before ** are handled correctly
  - Docs: README updated

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## 🐛 Bug Fix: LaTeX Commands with Letters

Fixed critical bug where LaTeX commands with capital letters were blocked by natural language detection.

**Previously broken:**
```markdown
( \angle DEF = 125^\circ )
( \angle ABC )
( \frac{1}{2} ab \sin C )
```

**Now works:** ✅ All convert to inline math correctly.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## 🐛 Bug Fix: Quoted Bracket Blocks Support

Fixed support for multi-line quoted bracket blocks in blockquotes from AI assistants.

### What's Fixed

**Previously broken:**
```markdown
> [
> \cos(\angle DEF) = \frac{-92.99}{163.48}
> ]
```

**Now works:** ✅ Converts to `> $$ ... $$`

Both `> [...]` and `> \[...\]` formats now work correctly in blockquotes.

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Version 1.0.13 - Bugfix Release

### 🐛 Bug Fix
**Fixed natural language detection threshold**: Mathematical expressions with 2-letter variables now convert correctly.

**Problem in v1.0.12:**
- `(y=ax^2+bx+c)` was incorrectly detected as text (because "ax" and "bx" are 2 letters) ❌
- `(a,b,c)` was not converting (comma was not recognized as operator) ❌
- Polynomial expressions in parentheses were ignored ❌

**Fixed in v1.0.13:**
- Changed minimum word length from 2 to 3 letters
- Added comma as mathematical operator (for variable lists)
- `(y=ax^2+bx+c)` → `$y=ax^2+bx+c$` ✅
- `(a,b,c)` → `$a,b,c$` ✅
- Better distinction between mathematical variables and natural language words
releases.name: Release 1.0.13

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
### 🔧 Improvements
- **Better UX**: Command is now disabled when no Markdown file is active
- **Performance**: Uses Obsidian's recommended `Vault.process` API for file operations

### 📦 Technical Changes
- Replaced `read` + `modify` with atomic `Vault.process` operation
- Implemented conditional command with `checkCallback`
- Refactored status bar update logic
- Improved error handling with proper TypeScript types

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Fixed
- Improved detection of algebraic expressions in brackets (e.g., `[n(k-n)]` now converts correctly)

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Changes
     - Fixed all ESLint errors for Obsidian plugin guidelines compliance
     - Improved UI text to follow sentence case conventions
     - Enhanced TypeScript strict mode compatibility
     
     ## Technical
     - Command naming follows Obsidian guidelines
     - Status bar messages use sentence case
     - All code passes ESLint with 0 errors

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
**Added**

LaTeX number formatting detection: Now recognises large numbers formatted with LaTeX separators

- Detects patterns like 123{,}456{,}789 (thousands separators)
- Detects patterns like 1{.}234 (decimal point formatting)
- Common in scientific and financial documents

**Fixed**
Large formatted numbers like (405{,}871{,}547{,}406{,}714) now convert to inline math

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
**Added**

Single-line bracket math detection: Now converts [ \frac{a}{b} = ... ] from AI assistants (Windows Copilot, ChatGPT) to display math

Intelligently filters out Markdown links [text](url), wikilinks [[page]], and footnotes [^ref]
Detects LaTeX commands (\frac, \int, etc.) or mathematical patterns
Handles inline bracket blocks that were previously ignored
releases.name: Release 1.0.8

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
**Added**

**Conversion statistics**: Now shows exact count of converted formulas (e.g., "Converted 5 formulas (3 inline, 2 block)")
**Status bar updates**: Status bar now displays conversion results and automatically resets after 5 seconds
**Improved math detection**: Now recognises simple variable equations like (x=y), (a<b), (f>g) without requiring digits
**Better notifications**: More informative messages that match the documented behaviour

**Changed**

Updated notification messages to be more descriptive and include statistics
The status bar now provides real-time feedback on conversion operations
Enhanced isMathy() heuristic to detect variable-only equations

**Fixed**

Math detection now correctly identifies expressions like (x=y) that were previously ignored
Status bar message consistency throughout the plugin lifecycle

**Documentation**

Updated README with comprehensive examples and feature documentation
Added a detailed explanation of what gets converted and what stays unchanged
Documented quoted block math support (e.g., > \[...\])
Added a technical details section explaining how the plugin works
releases.name: Release 1.0.7

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: - Quoted block formulas written as multi-line `> \[ ... \]` are now converted to single-line `> $$ ... $$` without stray `>` characters inside.
releases.name: Release 1.0.6

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Correct handling of nested parentheses, delimiters and trailing primes.
- Prevented mis-grouping in sentences, where previously the conversion produced incorrect mixed `$...$$...$` blocks.
- Inline rules are no longer applied inside block maths or inside newly created `$$ ... $$` sections.
releases.name: Release 1.0.5

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: Improve math-detection heuristics for inline parentheses
releases.name: Release 1.0.4

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
### Improvements
- Fixed incorrect inline math conversion inside block math.
- Improved detection for expressions like `(3x^{2}-3)` so that only the maths part is converted.
- Block maths (`$$ ... $$`) are now fully protected from inline re-processing.
releases.name: Release 1.0.3

releases.author: loglux
releases.name: Fix Math 1.0.2

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
This update improves formula detection and expands support beyond traditional LaTeX delimiters.

✨ New:

Added support for multiline math blocks using plain square brackets:

[
(x+3)(x+5)(x+7)(x+9)=9
]


→ automatically converted to:

$$
(x+3)(x+5)(x+7)(x+9)=9
$$

🛡️ Improved:

Added math detection heuristic (isMathy) to avoid converting normal text in brackets.

Removed lookbehind from regexes for full compatibility with all Obsidian versions.

Keeps existing support for:

\[ ... \] → $$ ... $$

\( ... \) → $ ... $

Still ignores content inside code fences.

🧪 Example:

Before:

[
(y-3)(y-1)(y+1)(y+3)=9
]


After running the command:

$$
(y-3)(y-1)(y+1)(y+3)=9
$$
releases.name: Release 1.0.1

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Initial release

Features:
- Convert \[...\] to $$...$$ (display math)
- Convert \(...\) to $...$ (inline math)
- Ribbon button for quick access
- Command palette integration
- Skips code blocks
releases.name: Release 1.0.0
